summary: Official Windows service control for Windows Search (WSearch). Microsoft documents the service name, start-mode baseline, feature behavior, and says it is OK to disable in the service guidance used here. The app disables the service by setting its start mode to Disabled.

setting.name: Windows Search Service
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Search builds and maintains an index so Start and Explorer searches can return results faster. Disabling it reduces indexing activity, but it also removes the indexed search experience.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they do not use Windows indexing, prefer third-party search tools, or want to reduce indexing-related background work.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning off the service can reduce indexing work, but Windows Search features lose indexed results and some experiences can become slower or unavailable.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists Windows Search `(WSearch)` with a documented start mode baseline and marks it `OK to disable`. Microsoft also documents the Windows Search indexing process and confirms the feature area and its role in indexed search results. The current app disables `WSearch` through a service start-mode tweak.

allowed_values.label: Microsoft documented baseline
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Search keeps the documented start-mode baseline from the service guidance used in this record.

allowed_values.label: Search service disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Indexed Windows Search is unavailable until the service start mode is restored.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables the WSearch service by setting its start mode to Disabled through the batch service tweak.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the Windows Search service guidance and feature model captured in this record

states.rationale: Keep the documented service baseline from the Microsoft service guidance used in this record.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline preserves indexed Windows Search behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Anyone relying on Start or Explorer search
avoid_for: Users who intentionally do not want indexing
recommended_profiles.good_when: You use Windows Search and want the normal indexed search experience.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You prefer third-party search tools and want indexing off.
tradeoffs: The service can still do indexing work when needed.

recommended_profiles.label: No search indexing
intended_for: Users who do not rely on Windows Search
Systems using Everything or other external search tools
avoid_for: Users who rely on indexed file or mail search
Systems where Search features must remain available

states.rationale: Disable the service to remove indexed search behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally do not want the Windows Search indexing service running.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want fast indexed search results in Windows.
tradeoffs: Windows Search features lose the indexed search experience.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Guidance on configuring system services - Windows Search
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists Windows Search `(WSearch)` with start mode `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`, describing it as providing content indexing, property caching, and search results.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Search indexing process overview
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the Windows Search indexing system and confirms the feature area and its role in indexed search results.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables the `WSearch` service through the batch service start-mode tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the WSearch service name, its Manual baseline, its search-indexing behavior, and explicitly marks it OK to disable in the service guidance used here. The app's service-start-mode action matches that documented control surface cleanly.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Windows Search (WSearch); Manual; Provides content indexing, property caching, and search results for files, e-mail, and other content.; OK to disable: This service provides functionality that is useful to some but not all enterprises, and security-focused enterprises that don't use it can safely disable it.
validation_proof.notes: The Microsoft services guidance page explicitly lists the WSearch service, documents its Manual baseline and behavior, and marks it OK to disable.
